mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 5.0% indicates a tightening labor market, which correlates with stronger corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with UNRATE < 5.0% show negative or neutral performance (mean return < 0.1% or t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals weak supply chain activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in periods with INDPRO below MA exceed 1.5% or show positive Rank IC (>0.05) in rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts above 1.15 million units signals strong consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods with HOUST > 1150.0 show negative or neutral performance (mean return < 0.1% or t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP and retail sales forecasts indicates robust demand, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through earnings optimism.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in periods with both GDPC1 and RSAFS growth show negative or neutral performance (mean return < 0.1% or t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A monetary tightening cycle, indicated by FEDFUNDS above 4.5%, leads to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with FEDFUNDS > 4.5% show positive or neutral performance (mean return > 0.1% or t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers expectations of rate hikes, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods with CPIAUCSL > 2.5% show positive or neutral performance (mean return > 0.1% or t-test p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with low unemployment signals a tight labor market, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through wage-driven consumption.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods with PAYEMS > 140k and UNRATE < 5.0% show negative or neutral performance (mean return < 0.1% or t-test p > 0.1).
